text:
A couple of handfuls of blackberries (optional)
Clotted cream or ice cream to serve

Peel the pears, leaving the stalks intact, and cut a thin sliver off the base of each one so they will stand upright.

Put the perry, cloves, cinnamon, peppercorns and caster sugar into a heavy-based saucepan and add the pears. Lay a piece of greaseproof paper over the fruit. Bring to a simmer and poach gently for about 45 minutes until the pears are soft and tender but still holding their shape.

Lift out the poached pears. Using a slotted spoon. And set aside on a plate.

Continue to simmer the liquor in the pan until 1t has reduced by about two thirds and t111ckened return the pears to the liquor and leave to cool

To serve, stand each pear 1n a deep scrvrng plate and spoon over some of the reduced liquor. Add a few blackberries, if using, and a scoop of clotted cream
Or ice cream.
